words.note: your wife's mother; husband's mother is svekrva

example.gloss: My mother-in-law cooks wonderfully.

example.target: Punac mi je pokazao svoj vinograd.
example.gloss: My father-in-law showed me his vineyard.

words.note: your husband's mother; wife's mother is punica

example.target: Ana se dobro slaže sa svekrvom.
example.gloss: Ana gets along well with her mother-in-law.
Her father-in-law lives in the countryside.

words.note: everyday word for any brother-in-law; from German Schwager

example.target: Šogor mi je pomogao oko selidbe.
example.gloss: My brother-in-law helped me with the move.
My sister-in-law is coming to dinner tomorrow.
The mother-in-law loves her son-in-law very much.
Their daughter-in-law often looks after the grandchildren.
My nephew is five years old.
My niece sent me a postcard.

words.note: child of your aunt or uncle

example.target: S bratićem igram nogomet svaki vikend.
example.gloss: I play football with my cousin every weekend.

words.note: child of your aunt or uncle

example.gloss: My cousin studies in Split.

words.note: colloquial synonym of sestrična, common in many regions

example.target: Rodica nam je došla u posjet.
example.gloss: Our cousin came to visit us.
Uncle Ivan is mum's brother.
My aunt bakes the best cakes.

words.note: specifically your father's brother; V striče

example.target: Stric Marko je tatin stariji brat.
example.gloss: Uncle Marko is dad's older brother.

example.target: Strina i stric imaju troje djece.
example.gloss: My aunt and uncle have three children.

words.note: your mother's or father's sister; also a friendly word for any adult woman (kids' speech)

example.target: Teta Marija nam uvijek donosi darove.
example.gloss: Aunt Marija always brings us presents.

words.note: husband of your aunt; G tetka

example.target: Tetak nas vozi na more svako ljeto.
example.gloss: Our uncle drives us to the seaside every summer.

words.note: general word for a relative; f. rođakinja

example.gloss: I have a lot of relatives in Slavonia.

example.target: On mi je daljnji rođak s mamine strane.
example.gloss: He is a distant relative of mine on my mother's side.
Grandpa and grandma are looking after their grandson.
The granddaughter looks like her grandmother.

words.note: f. praunuka; prefix pra- = great- (a generation further)

example.gloss: Great-grandma already has three great-grandchildren.
My great-grandmother is ninety years old.
My great-grandfather was born in Dalmatia.

example.target: Kumčetu sam za rođendan kupio slikovnicu.
example.gloss: I bought my godchild a picture book for their birthday.

words.note: female counterpart of kum; a very close family friend

example.target: Anina sestra bila je kuma na vjenčanju.
example.gloss: Ana's sister was the maid of honour at the wedding.

example.target: Imam polubrata iz očeva prvog braka.
example.gloss: I have a half-brother from my father's first marriage.
My half-sister lives with mum.

example.target: Maćeha ga je odgojila kao svoje dijete.
example.gloss: His stepmother raised him as her own child.

example.target: Očuh mu je kao pravi otac.
example.gloss: His stepfather is like a real father to him.

words.note: impf. posvajati; for pets use udomiti

example.gloss: They decided to adopt a child.

example.target: Posvojenje je trajalo gotovo dvije godine.
example.gloss: The adoption took almost two years.

example.target: Njihov brak traje već dvadeset godina.
example.gloss: Their marriage has lasted twenty years already.
The neighbours are a likeable married couple.
You may bring your spouse to the dinner.
My husband works at the hospital.
His wife is Croatian.

words.note: from oženiti se; for a woman: udana

example.target: Marko je oženjen već pet godina.
example.gloss: Marko has been married for five years.

words.note: also udata; for a man: oženjen

example.target: Moja sestra je udana i ima sina.
example.gloss: My sister is married and has a son.
The bride danced all night.

example.target: Mladoženja je nervozno čekao pred crkvom.
example.gloss: The groom waited nervously in front of the church.

example.target: Zaruke smo proslavili u krugu obitelji.
example.gloss: We celebrated the engagement with the family.
Her fiancé comes from Osijek.

example.target: Doveo je zaručnicu da upozna baku.
example.gloss: He brought his fiancée to meet grandma.
The proposal was a big surprise.

words.note: impf. prositi (which also means: to beg)

example.target: Zaprosio ju je na plaži u Dubrovniku.
example.gloss: He proposed to her on a beach in Dubrovnik.

words.note: impf. vjenčavati se; also oženiti se (man) / udati se (woman)

example.target: Vjenčali smo se prošlog ljeta u Zagrebu.
example.gloss: We got married last summer in Zagreb.
The wedding dress belonged to her grandmother.

example.target: Izgubio je vjenčani prsten na plaži.
example.gloss: He lost his wedding ring on the beach.

words.note: issued by the matični ured (registry office)

example.gloss: We need the marriage certificate for the visa.

example.target: Matičar nas je vjenčao u gradskoj vijećnici.
example.gloss: The registrar married us in the town hall.

words.note: plural only; the whole wedding procession

example.gloss: The wedding guests celebrated until morning.

example.target: Medeni mjesec proveli smo na Hvaru.
example.gloss: We spent our honeymoon on Hvar.
Tomorrow we celebrate our tenth wedding anniversary.

example.target: Za Božić imamo veliko obiteljsko okupljanje.
example.gloss: At Christmas we have a big family gathering.

words.note: Sunday lunch at the in-laws' is an institution in Croatia

example.target: Nedjeljom idemo punici na obiteljski ručak.
example.gloss: On Sundays we go to my mother-in-law's for family lunch.

words.note: upoznati (pf.) = to meet for the first time

example.gloss: Tomorrow I am going to meet her parents.

example.target: Oni su u vezi već tri godine.
example.gloss: They have been in a relationship for three years.

words.note: prekinuti (pf., impf. prekidati) = to break off

example.target: Ana i Marko prekinuli su vezu.
example.gloss: Ana and Marko broke up.
After a quarrel comes reconciliation.

example.target: Bila je to samo mala obiteljska svađa.
example.gloss: It was just a small family quarrel.

words.note: no pf.; takes dative: sličiti ocu = to look like one's father

example.gloss: Our son looks like his grandfather.
She inherited the house from her grandmother.

example.target: Razvod je bio težak za cijelu obitelj.
example.gloss: The divorce was hard on the whole family.

example.target: Razveli su se nakon deset godina braka.
example.gloss: They divorced after ten years of marriage.

example.target: Baka je udovica već pet godina.
example.gloss: Grandma has been a widow for five years.

example.target: Djed je ostao udovac prošle godine.
example.gloss: Grandpa became a widower last year.

example.target: Kao samohrani roditelj odgaja dvoje djece.
example.gloss: As a single parent she is raising two children.

example.target: Na krštenje dolazi cijela šira obitelj.
example.gloss: The whole extended family is coming to the christening.

example.target: Djeca u školi crtaju obiteljsko stablo.
example.gloss: The children are drawing a family tree at school.
She kept her maiden name.

example.target: Naši preci došli su iz Hercegovine.
example.gloss: Our ancestors came from Herzegovina.

example.target: On je potomak stare dubrovačke obitelji.
example.gloss: He is a descendant of an old Dubrovnik family.

words.note: G blizanca; f. blizanka; pl. blizanci

example.gloss: Ivan and Luka are twins.

example.target: Ivan je jedinac, nema braće ni sestara.
example.gloss: Ivan is an only child, he has no brothers or sisters.

example.target: U našem kućanstvu žive četiri osobe.
example.gloss: Four people live in our household.

words.note: pf. uzdržati; uzdržavati obitelj = to provide for a family

example.gloss: His parents still support him financially.

example.target: Roditeljstvo je najljepši posao na svijetu.
example.gloss: Parenthood is the most beautiful job in the world.
Their daughter came of age.

words.note: often biaspectual; pf. also pokrstiti; krštenje = christening

example.gloss: We will have the baby baptized in June.
My sister-in-law lent me her wedding dress.
My brother-in-law invited me to go fishing.

example.target: Djever je održao zdravicu na svadbi.
example.gloss: My brother-in-law gave a toast at the wedding.
The baby is sleeping in the stroller.
A newborn sleeps almost the whole day.
An infant eats every three hours.
The pregnancy went without problems.

example.target: Moja žena je trudna pet mjeseci.
example.gloss: My wife is five months pregnant.
A pregnant woman needs a lot of rest.
She gave birth to a healthy boy.

example.target: Naša kći rodila se u Zagrebu.
example.gloss: Our daughter was born in Zagreb.
The delivery lasted six hours.

example.target: U rodilištu smo proveli tri dana.
example.gloss: We spent three days in the maternity ward.

example.target: Ana je godinu dana na rodiljnom dopustu.
example.gloss: Ana is on maternity leave for a year.
We have to buy new diapers.

words.note: impf. previjati; previti bebu = change the baby

example.gloss: Can you change the baby?

example.target: U torbi uvijek imamo vlažne maramice.
example.gloss: We always have wet wipes in the bag.

example.target: Beba ne može zaspati bez dude.
example.gloss: The baby cannot fall asleep without the pacifier.
Warm up the milk in the bottle.

words.note: the standard term on packaging and at the pharmacy

example.target: Kupili smo adaptirano mlijeko u ljekarni.
example.gloss: We bought baby formula at the pharmacy.
The baby is eating carrot puree.

example.target: Majka doji bebu svaka tri sata.
example.gloss: The mother breastfeeds the baby every three hours.
I have to feed the kids before school.
We left the stroller in front of the shop.
We always drive the child in a car seat.
Dad is carrying the baby in the carrier.

example.target: Beba u hodalici juri po kuhinji.
example.gloss: The baby in the walker races around the kitchen.
The baby sleeps in its crib.
Put a clean onesie on the baby.
The baby's first tooth is coming in.
Put the toys away in the box!

example.target: Spava samo sa svojim plišanim medvjedićem.
example.gloss: He only sleeps with his teddy bear.
The little girl is playing with a doll.
We are doing a hundred-piece puzzle.

words.note: sg. kocka = cube, block; G pl. kocaka

example.gloss: Our son is building a tower out of blocks.
We read a picture book every evening.

example.target: Kupila sam joj bojanku i bojice.
example.gloss: I bought her a colouring book and crayons.

example.target: Djeca u vrtiću uče novu pjesmicu.
example.gloss: The kids at kindergarten are learning a new song.

example.target: Baka joj pjeva staru hrvatsku uspavanku.
example.gloss: Grandma sings her an old Croatian lullaby.

example.target: Tata svaku večer čita priču za laku noć.
example.gloss: Dad reads a bedtime story every evening.
Her favourite fairy tale is Cinderella.
They are allowed to watch one cartoon a day.

words.note: common venue for kids' birthday parties

example.gloss: We are celebrating the birthday at a kids' play centre.
The children are going down the slide.
Push me on the swing!
The children are digging in the sandbox.
We rode the merry-go-round.

example.target: Sin ide u vrtić od treće godine.
example.gloss: Our son has been going to kindergarten since he was three.

words.note: plural only; daycare for children under three

example.target: Beba kreće u jaslice na jesen.
example.gloss: The baby starts nursery in the autumn.

example.target: Odgojiteljica kaže da je danas bio dobar.
example.gloss: The preschool teacher says he was good today.

words.note: impf. upisivati; upisati dijete u vrtić

example.gloss: We enrolled our daughter in kindergarten.
I am going to a parent-teacher meeting tonight.
His school bag is too heavy.
We are raising the children bilingually.
Raising children is not an easy job.

words.note: pf. okupati; kupati se = to bathe, swim

example.gloss: We bathe the baby every evening.

words.note: impf. presvlačiti; presvući se = change one's own clothes

example.gloss: Change the baby, she is all wet.
It is hard to get this child to sleep.

words.note: impf. smirivati; smiriti se = to calm down (oneself)

example.gloss: Only mum can calm the baby down.
The baby naps after lunch.

words.note: pf. pomaziti; maziti se = to snuggle

example.target: Tata mazi bebu dok ne zaspi.
example.gloss: Dad cuddles the baby until she falls asleep.
Grandma kisses the grandchildren on the cheeks.
Blow grandma a kiss!

words.en: to babysit, look after for a while

example.gloss: Can you babysit the kids tonight?

example.target: Tražimo dadilju za dvije večeri tjedno.
example.gloss: We are looking for a babysitter for two evenings a week.

example.target: Beba je počela puzati u sedmom mjesecu.
example.gloss: The baby started crawling in her seventh month.

words.note: pf. only; a classic milestone verb

example.target: Naša kći prohodala je s godinu dana.
example.gloss: Our daughter started walking at one year old.

words.note: dječji vrtić, dječja soba, dječje igralište

example.target: U parku je novo dječje igralište.
example.gloss: There is a new children's playground in the park.
He spent his childhood on an island.
He grew up in a big family.
Our children are bilingual.

example.target: Dvojezičnost je velika prednost za dijete.
example.gloss: Bilingualism is a big advantage for a child.
Croatian is her mother tongue.

example.target: Njihov sin je miran i poslušan.
example.gloss: Their son is quiet and obedient.

words.note: impf. slušati; poslušati mamu = do as mum says

example.gloss: Listen to mum and put on your jacket!
The younger brother is very naughty.

example.target: Baka i djed razmazili su unuke.
example.gloss: Grandma and grandpa have spoilt the grandchildren.

example.target: Za kaznu ne smije gledati crtiće.
example.gloss: As punishment he is not allowed to watch cartoons.
Don't punish him, it's not his fault.

example.target: Nagradili smo je sladoledom za dobru ocjenu.
example.gloss: We rewarded her with ice cream for a good grade.
He received praise from the teacher.

example.target: Dijete se rasplakalo zbog izgubljene igračke.
example.gloss: The child burst into tears over a lost toy.
The baby screams when she is hungry.
A child's crying woke us up.

example.target: Dijete ima vrućicu i mora ležati.
example.gloss: The child has a fever and has to stay in bed.
Tomorrow we are going to the pediatrician for a check-up.

words.note: often biaspectual; docijepiti = give a booster; cjepivo = vaccine

example.gloss: We had the child vaccinated against the flu.

words.note: a big family event in Croatia

example.target: Slavimo kćerin prvi rođendan u subotu.
example.gloss: We are celebrating our daughter's first birthday on Saturday.

example.target: Marko je brižan otac dvoje djece.
example.gloss: Marko is a caring father of two.
One has to be gentle with a baby.

words.note: odan prijatelju = devoted to a friend (+ dat.)

example.gloss: She is a loyal friend.

example.target: Ne vjerujem mu, čini mi se neiskren.
example.gloss: I don't trust him, he seems insincere to me.
I appreciate your honesty, thank you.
Success made him arrogant.

words.note: G skromnog; also = modest in size (skromna plaća)

example.target: Iako je bogat, ostao je skroman.
example.gloss: Although he is rich, he has stayed modest.

example.target: Tvrdoglav je kao i njegov djed.
example.gloss: He is as stubborn as his grandfather.
Be persistent and you will succeed.
Children are naturally curious.
Our neighbour is a generous man.

example.target: Ne budi sebičan, podijeli s bratom!
example.gloss: Don't be selfish, share with your brother!

words.note: ljubomoran na koga = jealous of someone

example.gloss: He is jealous of his brother's success.
Jealousy can destroy a relationship.

words.note: also = enviable (zavidna razina = an enviable level)

example.gloss: Envious colleagues were spreading rumours.

example.target: Njezin uspjeh izazvao je zavist u uredu.
example.gloss: Her success provoked envy in the office.

example.target: Osoblje hotela bilo je vrlo susretljivo.
example.gloss: The hotel staff were very accommodating.

example.target: Konobar je bio drzak prema gostima.
example.gloss: The waiter was insolent towards the guests.
That boy is really rude.
Be polite to your elders.
His behaviour was impolite.
He stayed calm despite the problems.

example.target: U krizi je pokazala nevjerojatnu smirenost.
example.gloss: In the crisis she showed incredible composure.

words.note: osjetljiv na kritiku = sensitive to criticism

example.gloss: He is very sensitive to criticism.
After the divorce he felt vulnerable.

example.target: Na razgovoru za posao bio je samouvjeren.
example.gloss: He was self-confident at the job interview.

example.target: Kao dijete bila je plaha i tiha.
example.gloss: As a child she was timid and quiet.
He is shy around people he doesn't know.

example.target: On je povučen, ali drag čovjek.
example.gloss: He is reserved but a dear man.

example.target: Ana je društvena i puna energije.
example.gloss: Ana is sociable and full of energy.

example.target: Otac im je bio strog, ali pravedan.
example.gloss: Their father was strict but fair.

words.note: also of weather and taste: blaga zima, blagi okus

example.target: Ima blagu narav, nikad se ne ljuti.
example.gloss: He has a gentle temperament, he never gets angry.

example.target: Bio je grub prema mlađem bratu.
example.gloss: He was rough with his younger brother.

example.target: Domaćini su bili srdačni i gostoljubivi.
example.gloss: The hosts were warm and hospitable.

example.target: Djed mi je dao mudar savjet.
example.gloss: My grandfather gave me a wise piece of advice.
Our daughter is very imaginative.
He is careful when spending money.

example.target: Djetinjstvo mu je bilo sretno i bezbrižno.
example.gloss: His childhood was happy and carefree.

example.target: Budi razuman, ne troši sve odjednom.
example.gloss: Be sensible, don't spend everything at once.
He was cruel to animals.

words.note: feminine noun used for both sexes; also = cuckoo

example.gloss: I'm not a coward, I'm just cautious.
Despite everything she remained optimistic.
Don't be so pessimistic!
I am satisfied with my new job.
She is dissatisfied with the pay and the conditions.
I am grateful to you for the help.
I feel deep gratitude towards my parents.

example.target: U novom gradu osjećala se usamljeno.
example.gloss: In the new city she felt lonely.

example.target: Usamljenost je velik problem starijih ljudi.
example.gloss: Loneliness is a big problem for elderly people.

words.note: napetost = tension; napet film = a gripping film

example.gloss: He was tense before the exam.
On holiday we are completely relaxed.

example.target: Zašto si tako uzrujan? Smiri se.
example.gloss: Why are you so upset? Calm down.
She is distressed because of the bad news.

example.target: Nakon poraza svi su bili potišteni.
example.gloss: After the defeat everyone was dejected.

example.target: Održao je dirljiv govor na svadbi.
example.gloss: He gave a touching speech at the wedding.
He could not hide his rage.

example.target: Vijest je primila s dubokom žalošću.
example.gloss: She received the news with deep sorrow.

words.note: Sram me je. = I am ashamed.

example.target: Sram me je zbog moje pogreške.
example.gloss: I am ashamed of my mistake.

words.note: osjećaj krivnje = a feeling of guilt

example.gloss: He felt guilt over the lie.
What a relief, everyone is fine!
The audience did not hide its delight.
The result was a big disappointment.

words.note: čeznuti za čim = to long for something

example.gloss: She feels a longing for her homeland.

example.target: Liječnici su izrazili zabrinutost zbog gripe.
example.gloss: Doctors expressed concern about the flu.

example.target: S ponosom je gledao svoju djecu.
example.gloss: He watched his children with pride.
Hatred never solves anything.
Before exams she feels anxiety.

example.target: Niz obraz joj je kliznula suza.
example.gloss: A tear slid down her cheek.

example.target: U očaju je nazvao staru prijateljicu.
example.gloss: In despair he called an old friend.

example.target: Pokazali su veliko suosjećanje prema obitelji.
example.gloss: They showed great compassion towards the family.
She was thrilled with her birthday present.

words.note: impf. razočaravati se; razočarati se u koga

example.gloss: I was disappointed in him.

example.target: Djeca su se uzbudila zbog putovanja.
example.gloss: The children got excited about the trip.

words.note: pf. only in everyday use; ganut = moved, touched

example.target: Njezine riječi ganule su sve prisutne.
example.gloss: Her words moved everyone present.
She comforted her friend after the break-up.

words.note: pf. požaliti; žaliti za čim (+ instr.)

example.gloss: I regret nothing.
He regrets his words.

example.target: Oprostila mu je nakon iskrene isprike.
example.gloss: She forgave him after a sincere apology.
I hate getting up early on Mondays.
I admire her persistence.
Children should respect their elders.

words.note: no pf.; suosjećati s kim (+ instr.)

example.target: Suosjećam s tobom, znam kako ti je.
example.gloss: I feel for you, I know how you feel.

words.note: pf. zasmetati; + dat.: smeta mi buka

example.gloss: Does the loud music bother you?

example.target: Dogovorila sam termin kod zubara za utorak.
example.gloss: I made a dentist appointment for Tuesday.

example.target: Morao sam otkazati sastanak zbog gripe.
example.gloss: I had to cancel the meeting because of the flu.

example.target: Odgodili smo put za sljedeći tjedan.
example.gloss: We postponed the trip to next week.

example.target: Molim vas, potvrdite dolazak do petka.
example.gloss: Please confirm your attendance by Friday.

words.note: impf. podsjećati; podsjetiti koga na što

example.gloss: Remind me to buy milk.

words.note: impf. zakazivati; zakazati pregled = book a check-up

example.target: Zakazali smo pregled za ponedjeljak ujutro.
example.gloss: We scheduled the check-up for Monday morning.

example.target: Nije se javio, ostavila sam poruku.
example.gloss: He didn't answer, so I left a message.
On Saturdays I vacuum the whole flat.
The vacuum cleaner is broken again.

words.note: pf. obrisati; brisati prašinu = to dust

example.gloss: I am dusting the shelves.

words.note: G pl. metli/metala; pomesti = to sweep up

example.gloss: Take the broom and sweep the kitchen.
Wipe the table with a wet cloth.

example.target: Nestalo nam je deterdženta za rublje.
example.gloss: We have run out of laundry detergent.

words.note: glačati = to iron; colloquially pegla, peglati

example.gloss: Unplug the iron before going out!
Hang the coat on the hanger.
Put the plates in the dishwasher.
The socket in the bathroom doesn't work.

example.target: Prekidač za svjetlo je pokraj vrata.
example.gloss: The light switch is next to the door.
I forgot my phone charger.
I need an extension cord for the lamp.
I keep all my tools in the garage.
Hand me the hammer and the nails.

example.target: Za ovaj vijak trebaš križni odvijač.
example.gloss: For this screw you need a Phillips screwdriver.
We need four screws for the shelf.

example.target: Zabij čavao u zid za sliku.
example.gloss: Drive a nail into the wall for the picture.
I borrowed a ladder from the neighbour.

example.target: Popravak perilice koštao je sto eura.
example.gloss: The washing machine repair cost a hundred euros.
The repairman is coming tomorrow to fix the boiler.
The plumber replaced the old pipe.
We are calling an electrician about the socket.
The water heater takes half an hour to heat the water.

example.target: Radijator u spavaćoj sobi je hladan.
example.gloss: The radiator in the bedroom is cold.

example.target: Uključili smo grijanje jer je zahladilo.
example.gloss: We turned on the heating because it got cold.
Turn off the tap while you brush your teeth.
Water is leaking from under the washing machine.
The key got stuck in the lock.
I fixed the cup with glue.
Where are the paper scissors?
Grandma taught me to sew.

words.note: igla i konac = needle and thread

example.target: Trebam iglu i konac za gumb.
example.gloss: I need a needle and thread for the button.
I water the flowers every morning.

example.target: Na košulji je mrlja od kave.
example.gloss: There is a coffee stain on the shirt.

example.target: Pazi da ne proliješ sok po tepihu.
example.gloss: Careful not to spill the juice on the carpet.

example.target: Razbio sam čašu dok sam prao suđe.
example.gloss: I broke a glass while washing the dishes.

words.note: impf. prljati; uprljati se = to get oneself dirty

example.gloss: The child got mud on his T-shirt.

words.note: G najma; stan u najmu = rented flat; stanarina = the rent (money)

example.target: Stan je u najmu, nije naš.
example.gloss: The flat is rented, it isn't ours.
The landlord raised the rent.

example.target: Godinama smo bili podstanari u Zagrebu.
example.gloss: For years we were tenants in Zagreb.
In winter the utilities come to two hundred euros.
The electricity bill has arrived.

words.note: paid at the post office or bank

example.gloss: I paid the payment slip at the post office.

example.target: Selidba u novi stan je u subotu.
example.gloss: The move to the new flat is on Saturday.
How much does a haircut cost at your place?
I have to get a haircut before the wedding.
The suit is at the dry cleaner's.
The cobbler repaired my boots.
I took the dress to the tailor.

words.note: auto na servisu = car in for servicing

example.target: Auto je na servisu do petka.
example.gloss: The car is in for servicing until Friday.
The car has to pass the technical inspection.

example.target: Od studenog vozimo na zimskim gumama.
example.gloss: From November we drive on winter tyres.
I bought a bouquet at the florist's.
I get fresh chicken at the butcher's.

example.target: Tijesto za pizzu mora odstajati sat vremena.
example.gloss: Pizza dough has to rest for an hour.
The kids have cereal with milk for breakfast.
Beans and lentils are legumes.

example.target: Tjestenina je najbolja s umakom od rajčice.
example.gloss: Pasta is best with tomato sauce.
In winter we often cook vegetable stew.
With the meat you can choose a side dish.

example.target: Za predjelo smo uzeli pršut i sir.
example.gloss: For the starter we had prosciutto and cheese.

example.target: Glavno jelo bila je pečena janjetina.
example.gloss: The main course was roast lamb.
For dessert I recommend the crepes.

words.note: coastal word; in Zagreb and the north: gablec

example.target: U deset sati idemo na marendu.
example.gloss: At ten o'clock we go for a mid-morning snack.
We left the waiter a tip.
The meat is in the freezer.
The towels are still in the dryer.
Heat up the soup in the microwave.
Put the lid on the pot.
Do you have any pets?

example.target: U parku pas mora biti na povocu.
example.gloss: In the park the dog has to be on a leash.
We are taking the cat to the vet for vaccination.

words.en: to adopt (a pet), give a home
words.note: impf. udomljavati; for children use posvojiti

example.gloss: We adopted a dog from the shelter.
The hamster is sleeping in its cage.
I walk the dog twice a day.

example.target: Kupi pijesak za mačke u dućanu.
example.gloss: Buy cat litter at the shop.

words.note: standard in traffic and weather reports

example.target: Zbog magle je vidljivost na cesti smanjena.
example.gloss: Because of fog, visibility on the road is reduced.

words.note: gusta magla = thick fog; gust promet = heavy traffic

example.gloss: Thick fog covered the motorway.

words.note: kolona vozila = a line of vehicles

example.target: Na granici je kolona duga pet kilometara.
example.gloss: At the border there is a five-kilometre queue.

example.target: Promet kroz tunel odvija se usporeno.
example.gloss: Traffic through the tunnel is moving slowly.

words.note: naplata cestarine = toll collection; naplatne kućice = toll booths

example.target: Kod naplate cestarine stvaraju se kolone.
example.gloss: Queues are forming at the toll station.

example.target: Na graničnom prijelazu čeka se sat vremena.
example.gloss: There is an hour's wait at the border crossing.

example.target: Zbog nesreće nastao je dug zastoj.
example.gloss: Because of an accident, a long hold-up formed.

example.target: Radovi na cesti traju do kraja mjeseca.
example.gloss: The roadworks last until the end of the month.

example.target: U nesreći su sudjelovala tri vozila.
example.gloss: Three vehicles were involved in the accident.
No one was injured in the collision.

words.note: impf. upozoravati; upozoriti koga na što

example.target: Vozače su upozorili na jak vjetar.
example.gloss: Drivers were warned about strong wind.

example.target: Izdano je upozorenje zbog jakog vjetra.
example.gloss: A warning was issued because of strong wind.

example.target: Prometna nesreća dogodila se kod Zadra.
example.gloss: A traffic accident happened near Zadar.
Twenty performers are appearing at the festival.
Her performance delighted the audience.
A famous klapa group is performing tonight.

example.target: U gradu je ljeti mnogo događanja.
example.gloss: In summer there are many events in the city.

example.target: Manifestacija se održava svake godine u lipnju.
example.gloss: The event is held every year in June.

example.target: Muzej je lani primio milijun posjetitelja.
example.gloss: The museum received a million visitors last year.
The organizers expect a large number of guests.

example.target: Grad je priredio doček za sportaše.
example.gloss: The city put on a welcome for the athletes.
Zagreb will host the European championship.

words.note: pf. održati se; reflexive sense of održati

example.target: Sajam se održava svake subote na trgu.
example.gloss: The fair is held every Saturday on the square.

words.note: impf. namjenjivati; namijenjen komu = intended for

example.target: Radionica je namijenjena djeci od pet godina.
example.gloss: The workshop is intended for children aged five.
On the occasion of the holiday, entry is free.

words.note: povod za slavlje = cause for celebration

example.target: Obljetnica je bila povod za slavlje.
example.gloss: The anniversary was a cause for celebration.

example.target: Ovo vino čuvamo za posebne prigode.
example.gloss: We keep this wine for special occasions.

example.target: Na svečanoj večeri bilo je sto gostiju.
example.gloss: There were a hundred guests at the gala dinner.

example.target: Svečanost počinje u podne na glavnom trgu.
example.gloss: The ceremony begins at noon on the main square.

example.target: Grad je obilježio stotu obljetnicu muzeja.
example.gloss: The city marked the museum's hundredth anniversary.

words.note: impf. slaviti; proslaviti se = to become famous

example.gloss: The club celebrated winning the title.

words.en: on the eve of, just before

example.gloss: Just before the holidays, shops stay open longer.

example.target: Tijekom ljeta očekuje se pojačan promet.
example.gloss: Heavier traffic is expected during the summer.

words.note: + genitive: diljem svijeta = all over the world

example.gloss: Concerts are being held throughout Croatia.

example.target: Raste broj inozemnih gostiju na Jadranu.
example.gloss: The number of foreign guests on the Adriatic is growing.

example.target: U utrci je bilo tisuću sudionika.
example.gloss: There were a thousand participants in the race.

example.target: Naša škola organizira natjecanje iz matematike.
example.gloss: Our school is organizing a mathematics competition.

example.target: Natječe se u plivanju od djetinjstva.
example.gloss: She has competed in swimming since childhood.
The winner of the competition receives a cash prize.

example.target: Štetu su procijenili na milijun eura.
example.gloss: They estimated the damage at a million euros.

example.target: Prema procjenama, došlo je deset tisuća ljudi.
example.gloss: According to estimates, ten thousand people came.

example.target: Plaća mu je iznad državnog prosjeka.
example.gloss: His salary is above the national average.

example.target: Prosječna temperatura porasla je za dva stupnja.
example.gloss: The average temperature rose by two degrees.
A ticket costs approximately twenty euros.
Prices will allegedly rise again.

example.target: Velik postotak mladih živi s roditeljima.
example.gloss: A large percentage of young people live with their parents.
An increase in the number of tourists was recorded.

words.note: pad cijena = a drop in prices

example.gloss: A drop in prices is expected in the autumn.
The unions are demanding a pay increase.
A reduction in electricity prices has been announced.

example.target: Udio žena u politici i dalje raste.
example.gloss: The share of women in politics keeps growing.

example.target: Stopa nezaposlenosti pala je na pet posto.
example.gloss: The unemployment rate fell to five percent.

example.target: Prema anketi, većina podržava novi zakon.
example.gloss: According to the survey, the majority supports the new law.
A third of respondents do not trust the media.
The minister emphasized the importance of education.

example.target: Postignut je značajan napredak u pregovorima.
example.gloss: Significant progress was made in the negotiations.

words.note: pf. iznijeti; cijena iznosi... = the price is...

example.gloss: The fine amounts to three hundred euros.

example.target: Trener je izjavio da je zadovoljan rezultatom.
example.gloss: The coach stated that he was satisfied with the result.

example.target: Vlada je najavila nove mjere štednje.
example.gloss: The government announced new austerity measures.

example.target: Mediji su izvijestili o velikom požaru.
example.gloss: The media reported on a large fire.
The club published an official statement.

words.note: + locative; very common in media Croatian

example.target: Riječ je o vrlo staroj tradiciji.
example.gloss: It concerns a very old tradition.

words.note: no pf.; svrbi me = it itches (me)

example.target: Ruka me svrbi od uboda komarca.
example.gloss: My arm itches from a mosquito bite.

example.target: Nakon jagoda dijete je dobilo osip.
example.gloss: After the strawberries the child got a rash.
Doctors recommend more exercise.

words.note: pf. posavjetovati; savjetovati se = to consult

example.gloss: Experts advise regular exercise.
Lack of sleep reduces concentration.

example.target: Maslina je rasprostranjena duž cijele obale.
example.gloss: The olive tree is widespread along the whole coast.
Workers removed the fallen tree.

example.target: Grad je pokrenuo novi projekt za mlade.
example.gloss: The city launched a new project for young people.

words.en: to arrange, do up; to edit

example.target: Uredili su park u središtu grada.
example.gloss: They did up the park in the city centre.

words.note: no pf.; boriti se protiv čega / za što

example.gloss: She has been fighting injustice for years.

example.target: Borba protiv požara trajala je cijelu noć.
example.gloss: The fight against the fire lasted all night.

example.target: Cijenim sve što ste učinili za nas.
example.gloss: I appreciate everything you have done for us.
The exhibition shows the work of young artists.

example.target: Metar je mjerna jedinica za duljinu.
example.gloss: The metre is a unit of measurement for length.

example.target: Ostatak dana proveli smo na plaži.
example.gloss: We spent the rest of the day on the beach.

words.note: pf. provesti; provoditi vrijeme = to spend time

example.target: Vikende provodimo kod punice na selu.
example.gloss: We spend weekends at my mother-in-law's in the countryside.

words.note: impf. prijavljivati; prijaviti se = to sign up

example.gloss: You can report the fault online.

example.target: Stipendija joj je omogućila studij u inozemstvu.
example.gloss: The scholarship enabled her to study abroad.

example.target: Film ga je potaknuo na razmišljanje.
example.gloss: The film prompted him to think.
The festival attracted numerous visitors.
The number of users of the app keeps growing.
The library is a public cultural institution.
